title: AI Scaling Laws & Emergence

analysis.object: T220: AI scaling laws and emergence under Universal EML-2 Theorem

chinchilla_scaling.description: L(N,D) = E + A/N^α + B/D^β (Hoffmann et al.)
chinchilla_scaling.reason: Power law scaling — log-log linear; EML-2 structure

grokking.description: Sudden generalization after many steps on algorithmic tasks
grokking.reason: Phase transition — discontinuous, no finite-step prediction

emergent_abilities.reason: Emergent = phase transition = EML-∞ critical point

loss_landscape.description: Training loss surface in parameter space
loss_landscape.reason: High-dimensional landscape with oscillatory saddle points

in_context_learning.reason: Implicit Bayesian inference via attention = oscillatory integration

analysis.universal_eml2_application: Universal EML-2 Theorem: all measurement processes (including generalization) are EML-2. Chinchilla scaling: L ~ N^{-α} is EML-2 (power law measurement of capacity). Grokking: the JUMP from memorization to generalization is EML-∞ → EML-2. The Δd=2 Theorem explains grokking: the network drops from EML-∞ (memorization) to EML-2 (generalization) in one depth jump. This predicts: grokking delay is proportional to the EML depth gap being bridged.

analysis.verdict: Scaling laws: EML-2. Emergence/grokking: EML-∞ → EML-2 phase transition.
analysis.theorem: T220: AI Scaling Depth — scaling is EML-2; grokking = Δd jump from EML-∞ to EML-2

key_theorem: T220: AI Scaling Depth (S499). Chinchilla/neural scaling: power law = EML-2. Grokking: memorization (EML-∞) → generalization (EML-2) = Δd jump. Δd=2 Theorem predicts: grokking delay ∝ depth gap being bridged. Emergent abilities = EML-∞ critical points, not continuous improvements.
rabbit_hole_log: L(N) ~ N^{-α}: power law = EML-2 measurement
Grokking: EML-∞ (memorize) → EML-2 (generalize) in one jump
Δd=2: predicts grokking delay proportional to depth gap
Emergence: abilities appear at EML-∞ critical points
T220: AI scaling IS EML-2; grokking = Δd structural jump
